document.source_title: Gaza risks ‘permanent’ state of limbo if transition plan stalls, Security Council hears
document.summary: The UN Security Council has been informed that the situation in the Occupied Palestinian Territory is increasingly precarious, with violence escalating in Gaza and the West Bank. Ramiz Alakbarov highlighted the dire humanitarian conditions in Gaza, where nearly a million people require urgent shelter assistance. The implementation of resolution 2803, which endorses a US peace plan, is delayed, risking a permanent state of limbo for the region. The need for urgent action to prevent further deterioration and to support a two-State solution was emphasized.
key_takeaways: The humanitarian crisis in Gaza is severe, with nearly a million people needing urgent shelter.
Resolution 2803, which supports a US peace plan, is not being implemented, causing delays.
80% of buildings in Gaza are damaged or destroyed, leaving many without permanent shelter.
Israeli settlement expansion in the West Bank is intensifying, with over 2,200 new housing units planned.
The ceasefire has not yet brought safety to Gaza, with ongoing violence and humanitarian struggles.
Urgent progress is needed to avoid a permanent state of limbo and to support a two-State solution.
document.why_it_matters: The situation in Gaza and the West Bank is critical, with escalating violence and humanitarian needs. The failure to implement peace resolutions risks entrenching a cycle of conflict and suffering, making it imperative for international actors to engage in meaningful dialogue and support for a sustainable resolution.

claims.claim_text: The humanitarian conditions in Gaza remain severe.
claims.evidence_text: Nearly a million people across the enclave still need urgent shelter assistance.

claims.claim_text: Resolution 2803 is delayed, impacting peace efforts.
claims.evidence_text: Delays in the implementation of resolution 2803, alongside daily violence and a continuing humanitarian crisis, have replaced the early momentum following the ceasefire.
Israeli planning authorities had recently advanced plans for more than 2,200 new settlement housing units.

entities.name: UN
entities.description: The United Nations, involved in peacekeeping and humanitarian efforts in the region.
entities.evidence_text: Ramiz Alakbarov, UN Deputy Special Coordinator for the Middle East Peace Process, said the situation across the Occupied Palestinian Territory was becoming 'increasingly precarious'.

entities.name: Hamas
entities.description: A Palestinian militant organization that governs the Gaza Strip.
entities.evidence_text: Mr. Mladenov stressed that Hamas and all armed groups must eventually be disarmed under Palestinian authority.

entities.name: Palestine Red Crescent Society
entities.description: A humanitarian organization providing medical services in Palestine.
entities.evidence_text: The Council also heard an account from Rami Hijjo of the Palestine Red Crescent Society.

events.event: UN Security Council meeting on the situation in the Middle East
events.evidence_text: The UN Security Council has been informed that the situation in the Occupied Palestinian Territory is increasingly precarious.

metrics.name: Funding for UN-coordinated Flash Appeal
metrics.evidence_text: The UN-coordinated 2026 Flash Appeal, which seeks more than $4 billion to support nearly three million people across Gaza and the West Bank, is only around 13 per cent funded.

citations.title: Gaza risks ‘permanent’ state of limbo if transition plan stalls, Security Council hears

source_credibility.reason: The information is sourced from a UN Security Council meeting, which is a credible and authoritative source.
